name: pptx-official
description: Presentation creation, editing, and analysis. When Claude needs to work with presentations (.pptx files) for: (1) Creating new presentations, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with layo...
capabilities: Create new PowerPoint presentations from HTML markup with precise positioning and styling
Edit existing PPTX files by unpacking, modifying XML, and repacking with validation
Extract text, comments, speaker notes, and design elements from presentations
Analyze presentation structure including slide layouts, masters, and themes
Generate thumbnail previews for visual validation of slide layouts
Convert between HTML slides and PowerPoint format using PptxGenJS library
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged 1158 patterns but most are false positives. Markdown backticks flagged as shell commands, XSD schema enumerations flagged as C2 keywords, and standard library operations flagged as cryptographic risks. Actual risk is low: subprocess calls use hardcoded arguments for document conversion tools (soffice, pdftoppm). File operations are standard OOXML processing workflows. No user input injection vectors detected.
valueStatement: Transform how you work with PowerPoint files. Create professional presentations from HTML or edit existing PPTX files at the XML level with complete control over slides, layouts, and content.
limitations: Requires Node.js and Python runtime environments with specific dependencies installed
HTML conversion workflow requires Playwright browser automation for rendering
Thumbnail generation requires LibreOffice (soffice) and poppler utilities
Complex animations and transitions may not survive XML editing workflows

useCases.targetUser: Business analysts and project managers who need to produce recurring presentations
useCases.title: Automated Report Generation
useCases.description: Generate weekly status presentations from structured data by converting HTML templates to professionally formatted PPTX files with charts and tables.

useCases.targetUser: IT support staff and document specialists managing enterprise presentation libraries
useCases.title: Presentation Remediation
useCases.description: Fix corrupted presentations, extract content from damaged files, or batch-update branding across multiple slide decks using direct XML manipulation.

useCases.targetUser: Marketing teams and designers who maintain brand consistency across presentations
useCases.title: Design-Consistent Slide Creation
useCases.description: Create presentations that follow specific brand guidelines by defining color palettes, typography, and layout patterns in HTML before conversion.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Slide Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new PowerPoint presentation with 5 slides about [TOPIC]. Use a clean professional design with a blue color scheme. Include a title slide, agenda, 3 content slides with bullet points, and a summary slide.

promptTemplates.title: HTML to PowerPoint Conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert the following HTML slide deck to PowerPoint format. Preserve all styling, colors, and layouts. Add a bar chart to slide 3 showing quarterly revenue data: Q1: 120K, Q2: 145K, Q3: 138K, Q4: 162K.
promptTemplates.scenario: Converting web content or HTML prototypes to presentations

promptTemplates.title: Presentation Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this presentation file and provide: 1) Total slide count including hidden slides, 2) Color palette used in the theme, 3) Font families used throughout, 4) List of all comments and speaker notes, 5) Any slides with layout issues.
promptTemplates.scenario: Understanding existing presentation structure and content

promptTemplates.title: XML-Level Editing
promptTemplates.prompt: Open this PPTX file and make the following edits: 1) Update the company logo on all master slides, 2) Replace all instances of the old product name with the new one, 3) Add transition effects between section slides, 4) Export a validation report showing any XML errors fixed.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced editing requiring direct OOXML manipulation

bestPractices: Always validate XML after editing PPTX files before repacking to prevent corruption
Use the html2pptx workflow for new presentations to ensure accurate positioning and styling
Create thumbnail previews after generation to catch layout issues before delivery
Back up original files before performing XML-level edits on existing presentations
antiPatterns: Never manually edit XML without running validation - always use validate.py after changes
Avoid using non-web-safe fonts in HTML slides as they may not render correctly in PowerPoint
Do not place charts or tables in single-column vertical layouts - use two-column or full-slide layouts instead
Never skip the visual validation step - always generate thumbnails to verify slide appearance

faq.question: What dependencies do I need to install?
faq.answer: You need Node.js with pptxgenjs and playwright packages, plus Python 3 with defusedxml and lxml. For thumbnail generation, install LibreOffice and poppler-utils.

faq.question: Can this skill edit existing PowerPoint templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can unpack template PPTX files, analyze their structure, duplicate slides, and modify content while preserving the original design and layouts.

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: Primary support is for .pptx (PowerPoint). The underlying OOXML tools also support .docx (Word) and .xlsx (Excel) for document editing workflows.

faq.question: How do I handle charts and graphs?
faq.answer: For new presentations, define chart placeholder areas in HTML then use PptxGenJS to add chart data. For existing files, edit chart XML directly in the unpacked structure.

faq.question: Can this skill extract speaker notes?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can read notesSlides/notesSlide{N}.xml files to extract speaker notes, and can also add or modify notes during the editing process.

faq.question: What if the generated slides have layout issues?
faq.answer: Use the thumbnail generation feature to visually identify problems. Common fixes include adjusting HTML margins, ensuring proper flexbox layouts, and verifying text doesn't overflow slide boundaries.
